scenario.background: I just got out of the hospital

turns.content: I just got out of the hospital and I'm feeling really down. I was wondering if there's a place where I can take a shower and get cleaned up. It's been a while since I've had a proper bath.

I'm so sorry to hear that you're going through a tough time. It's great that you're thinking about your hygiene and well-being. We have a community center nearby that offers showers and laundry facilities. Would you like me to help you find out more about it?
That sounds amazing, thank you. But what about my mental health? I've been struggling with some stuff and I don't know who to turn to.

I'm glad you're thinking about your mental health. We have a social worker at the community center who can offer you support and connect you with resources. Would you like me to make an appointment for you?
That would be great, thank you. But what if I need help right away?

If you need help right away, please call emergency services immediately. But if you're looking for ongoing support, the community center is a great place to start. We can also connect you with a crisis hotline if you need someone to talk to.

scenario.background: I left my housing due to debt

turns.content: I just got kicked out of my housing and I'm standing here in the rain. I'm furious about it. I need help with my official documents, I don't know how to deal with this. Can you help me?

I'm so sorry to hear that you're going through this. It sounds like a really tough situation. Can you tell me a bit more about what happened with your housing and what kind of documents you need help with?
I just couldn't pay the rent anymore because of debt. I need to get my eviction notice and my rental agreement sorted out. And maybe some advice on what to do next?

Okay, let's take a step back and prioritize getting you into a safe and dry place. Have you considered reaching out to the municipality for assistance with housing? We can also help you get in touch with a social services organization that can provide you with some guidance on your situation. And I can try to connect you with a lawyer who can offer some advice on your eviction notice and rental agreement.
Yeah, I guess that sounds like a good idea. But what about my documents? I need to get those sorted out ASAP.

Don't worry, we can work on getting your documents in order. In the meantime, let's focus on getting you into a safe place. I can help you find a local shelter or community center where you can stay for the night. And we can follow up on getting your documents sorted out as soon as possible.

turns.content: I'm so exhausted, I've been walking around Charlois all night trying to find a place to store my belongings. I lost contact with my family a while back and I have nowhere to go. I just need a safe place to put my stuff for now.
I'm so sorry to hear that you're going through this. It sounds like you're in a really tough spot. Can you tell me a bit more about your situation and what you're looking for in terms of help?
Like I said, I just need a place to store my belongings for now. I also have some official documents that I need to sort out, but I don't know where to start.

There are a few options in Charlois that might be able to help you. We have a community center that offers storage for belongings, and they also have a social worker on staff who can help you with official documents. Would you like me to make a call to see if they have any availability?

If the community center can't help, we also have a shelter in the area that might be able to provide you with a safe place to stay, and they can also connect you with social services to help with your documents.
